flashcards.front: What is the primary advantage of NewSQL in-memory databases over traditional RDBMS?
flashcards.back: Combines ACID compliance with horizontal scalability while achieving sub-millisecond response times through in-memory processing.

flashcards.front: Name three key applications of NewSQL in-memory databases.
flashcards.back: 1. Real-time analytics 2. High-frequency trading 3. IoT data processing

flashcards.front: How do NewSQL systems handle data persistence with in-memory storage?
flashcards.back: Use write-ahead logging (WAL) and periodic snapshots to maintain durability while keeping active datasets in RAM.

flashcards.front: What hardware advancement enabled modern NewSQL systems?
flashcards.back: Affordable high-capacity RAM and NVMe storage for fast data recovery.

flashcards.front: What is the CAP theorem trade-off in NewSQL systems?
flashcards.back: Typically prioritize Consistency and Partition Tolerance (CP) while maintaining high Availability through efficient consensus protocols.

flashcards.front: How does sharding work in NewSQL databases?
flashcards.back: Automatic horizontal partitioning of data across multiple nodes while maintaining single logical database view.

flashcards.front: What is the role of MVCC in NewSQL systems?
flashcards.back: Multiversion Concurrency Control enables high-throughput transactions without locking through versioned data snapshots.
Ability to handle both OLTP and OLAP workloads simultaneously on same dataset, a key feature of NewSQL.

flashcards.front: How do NewSQL systems achieve linear scalability?
flashcards.back: Through shared-nothing architecture and automatic data redistribution across commodity servers.
